notes: Two features, separate code paths. (1) MASTER NAMESPACE HINT — when a Master is loaded, the cabinet picker now reads the map's own naming convention instead of a generic placeholder: the input placeholder uses a real sample cabinet from the file, and the empty box shows an 'IN THIS MASTER' panel listing each cabinet prefix with its rack count and ID range (e.g. 's1 · 342 racks · s1:001 – s1:342'). Each prefix is a tappable chip (reuses .master-suggestion) that fills the box with 'prefix:' and fires the existing number suggestions — no more cracking open the Master to learn the rack naming. Pure read of racksByCab keys. (2) METAL MATCH (Vendor Audit Suite ship 2 of 2) — scanning an asset/serial on the SCAN bench now looks it up against the loaded Master and shows a verdict chip on the collection row: green '✓ <loc · model>' (found / belongs at), gold '? NOT IN MASTER' (unknown / extra unit), cyan 'DUP' (already scanned). Lazy generation-gated serial+hostname index over racksByCab hosts, persisted compactly to phantom_audit_index_v1 so it survives a reload even if the full Master blob did not; returns no chip when the Master carries no serials (honest, no false 'unknown'). Wires into ScanDispatch's existing 'asset' classification — no new SCAN surface, no routing change. Zero new CSS rules (reuses .master-suggestion + inline styles; brace baseline 3122/3122). Three stamps at v1.6.78.
